description: Yasothon Province was created by Announcement of the Revolutionary Council No. 70 dated 6 February 1972, splitting Yasothon from Ubon Ratchathani Province. Effective 1 March 1972. The Revolutionary Council Announcement was the equivalent of a Royal Decree during the Thanom-era military government.
authoritative_source: Announcement of the Revolutionary Council No. 70 (6 February 1972), Wikisource transcription linked from Wikipedia 'Yasothon province'; verified at established_years.csv tis1099_code=35 (CONFIRMED grade).
notes: Citation reused from data/established_years.csv (CONFIRMED). Child-district list deferred — Royal Gazette transcription on Wikisource carries the seven amphoe enumeration in Thai script for future expansion.

description: Phayao Province was separated from Chiang Rai Province by the Phayao Province Act, B.E. 2520 (พระราชบัญญัติตั้งจังหวัดพะเยา พ.ศ. 2520). Royal Gazette publication B.E. 2520. Effective 28 August 1977. Phayao was Thailand's 72nd province.
authoritative_source: Phayao Province Act, B.E. 2520, Royal Gazette publication; verified at established_years.csv tis1099_code=56 (CONFIRMED grade); Wikipedia 'Phayao province' History section.
notes: Citation reused from data/established_years.csv (CONFIRMED). Phayao Province Act is a defined legal instrument; child-district enumeration deferred pending Royal Gazette index lookup.

description: Mukdahan Province was elevated from a district of Nakhon Phanom Province to provincial status by Royal Decree, with Royal Gazette publication in 1982. Effective 27 September 1982. Mukdahan was Thailand's 73rd province.
authoritative_source: Royal Decree (Royal Gazette publication, B.E. 2525); verified at established_years.csv tis1099_code=49 (CONFIRMED grade); Wikipedia 'Mukdahan province' History section.
notes: Citation reused from data/established_years.csv (CONFIRMED). Child-district list deferred pending Royal Gazette index lookup for the constituent amphoe at the date of the split.

description: Bueng Kan province was created by Royal Decree on 2011-03-23 from eight districts previously part of Nong Khai province. Pre-2011 datasets that join on Nong Khai's then-larger territory should aggregate Bueng Kan and Nong Khai for any pre-2011 comparison.
authoritative_source: Royal Gazette publication of the Royal Decree creating Bueng Kan Province (พระราชบัญญัติตั้งจังหวัดบึงกาฬ พ.ศ. 2554); Wikipedia 'Bueng Kan Province' historical section
notes: Founding entry for the historical-mappings table. Eight districts moved from Nong Khai to Bueng Kan: Bueng Kan (district), Pak Khat, So Phisai, Seka, Phon Charoen, Si Wilai, Bung Khla, Bueng Khong Long. The TIS-1099 code 38 was assigned to Bueng Kan and the prior occupant of code 38 was renumbered. Consult original Royal Decree for precise district list and effective dates.
